Conerman later wrote that man has two systems of thought. The first is our animal mind that is fast, instinctive, and emotional, and it's in gear all the time. This is also sometimes referred to as the limbic system we've all heard of the amygdala, other parts of the brain with Latin sounding names that no one can remember, but a great analogy is an analogy to the respiratory system. Our respiratory system is working all the time, whether we're awake, whether we're asleep. You can interfere with your respiratory system briefly, the same way you can interfere with your animal mind briefly, but it's going to kick back into gear and go back on autopilot whether you like it or not. The second system is slow, calculating, and logical. It's not in gear all the time. The animal mind influences our rational thoughts. Now whether the animal mind is first or second, it doesn't matter because it's always there. It's never not part of our thinking. You can think of it as the spring that feeds the river. So if you know how to affect someone's animal mind thinking, then you can guide their rational mind and therefore modify their responses. And you know what? We've got tactics designed to trigger both systems, both minds, in very specific ways. Use your newfound powers for good and not evil. I'm not kidding.
